creator: Sean O'Kane
title: (No AI) SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO
dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
content: The deal is supposed to help SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI.
contentSnippet: The deal is supposed to help SpaceX's struggling AI division. The company told IPO investors it sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI.

creator: Kate Park
title: (No AI) Malaysia’s AI agent-powered messaging app Respond.io raises $62.5M, eyes acquisitions
dc:creator: Kate Park
content: Respond.io, one of Malaysia startups to watch, uses AI agents to handle high volumes of customer inquiries and charges per convo, not per seat.
contentSnippet: Respond.io, one of Malaysia startups to watch, uses AI agents to handle high volumes of customer inquiries and charges per convo, not per seat.

title: (No AI) SpaceX is officially buying Cursor for $60 billion
author: Robert Hart
content:
![Elon Musk with big green arrow pointing up and money falling from the sky.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/STKE012_SPACEX_IPO_2026_F.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Days after its [massive IPO](https://www.theverge.com/business/948996/spacex-ipo-elon-musk), SpaceX says it is spending $60 billion to buy Cursor - a bet designed to help Elon Musk's sprawling rocket / AI / social media behemoth win over lucrative enterprise customers and close the gap with AI rivals like Anthropic and OpenAI.

The takeover was not entirely unexpected: SpaceX announced a [peculiar arrangement](https://www.theverge.com/science/916427/spacex-cursor-potential-deal-acquisition) in April in which it agreed to either acquire the programming platform for $60 billion or pay a $10 billion breakup fee. The company had been holding off completing the deal while going public.

In an [SEC filing](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026043411/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm), SpaceX said it expects the deal to close during the third quarter of 2026.

Musk has pr …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/950571/spacex-is-officially-buying-cursor-for-60-billion)
contentSnippet:
Days after its massive IPO, SpaceX says it is spending $60 billion to buy Cursor - a bet designed to help Elon Musk's sprawling rocket / AI / social media behemoth win over lucrative enterprise customers and close the gap with AI rivals like Anthropic and OpenAI.
The takeover was not entirely unexpected: SpaceX announced a peculiar arrangement in April in which it agreed to either acquire the programming platform for $60 billion or pay a $10 billion breakup fee. The company had been holding off completing the deal while going public. 
In an SEC filing, SpaceX said it expects the deal to close during the third quarter of 2026. 
Musk has pr …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Inside the fight over Claude Mythos 5
author: Hayden Field
content:
![Collage of the Pentagon in Washington DC with binary code in the background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STKS515_CYBER_WARFARE2_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

As the rest of the country celebrated the USA's first World Cup win and the New York Knicks championship, Anthropic spent its weekend fighting the Trump administration over its latest model release. At 5:21 PM on Friday, the company received a US export control directive to suspend access to its Mythos 5 and Fable 5 AI models by "any foreign national" inside or outside the US, "including foreign national Anthropic employees." The only way that was possible, Anthropic determined, was to completely [disable](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949553/anthropic-fable-5-mythos-5-government-national-security) products it spent the [past week hyping](https://www.theverge.com/news/946725/anthropic-releases-claude-fable-5-mythos) - and travel to Washington, DC in hopes of changing President Donald Trump's mind. Now, over the com …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/950412/anthropic-trump-adminstration-claude-mythos-fable-5-export-controls)

The only way that was possible, Anthropic determined, was to completely disable products it spent the past week hyping - and travel to Washington, DC in hopes of changing President Donald Trump's mind. Now, over the com …

title: (No AI) Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network
content: OpenAI launches the Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.
contentSnippet: OpenAI launches the Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.

title: (No AI) New OpenAI Academy courses for the next era of work
content: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.
contentSnippet: OpenAI introduces three Academy courses that help people build practical AI skills, create repeatable workflows, and apply agents in everyday work.

title: (No AI) Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
content: Google DeepMind and partners announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent safety research.
contentSnippet: Google DeepMind and partners announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent safety research.

creator: Paresh Dave
title: (No AI) ‘Pretty Crazy’ Token Usage Is Testing Bosses’ Bet on AI

content: A Silicon Valley software maker and an ecommerce company reveal to WIRED how they are navigating the emerging challenge of “tokenomics.”

creator: Molly Taft
title: (No AI) DOJ Lawyers Argue xAI Is ‘Vital’ for National Security in NAACP Lawsuit
dc:creator: Molly Taft
content: In a bid to dismiss a lawsuit over xAI’s polluting gas turbines, the Justice Department claimed the company is integral to military operations—including the Iran War.
contentSnippet: In a bid to dismiss a lawsuit over xAI’s polluting gas turbines, the Justice Department claimed the company is integral to military operations—including the Iran War.
